content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. For an integer KKK, let's define a KKK-compressed sequence B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã as follows:


for each valid iii, BiB_iBi‚Äã is a positive integer
for each valid iii, if there are exactly XXX numbers smaller than or equal to AiA_iAi‚Äã in the subsequence Amax(1,i‚àíK),‚Ä¶,Amin(N,i+K)A_{\mathop{max}(1, i-K)}, \dots, A_{\mathop{min}(N, i+K)}Amax(1,i‚àíK)‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Amin(N,i+K)‚Äã, then there must be exactly XXX numbers smaller than or equal to BiB_iBi‚Äã in the subsequence Bmax(1,i‚àíK),‚Ä¶,Bmin(N,i+K)B_{\mathop{max}(1, i-K)}, \dots, B_{\mathop{min}(N, i+K)}Bmax(1,i‚àíK)‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Bmin(N,i+K)‚Äã
B1+B2+‚ãØ+BNB_1 + B_2 + \dots + B_NB1‚Äã+B2‚Äã+‚ãØ+BN‚Äã is minimum possible
You may notice that for K=N‚àí1K = N-1K=N‚àí1 or K=NK = NK=N, this becomes the well-known technique of coordinate compression.

For example, consider the sequence A=[4,2,8,1,4,3,8,1]A = [4, 2, 8, 1, 4, 3, 8, 1]A=[4,2,8,1,4,3,8,1]. If we choose K=1K = 1K=1, then the KKK-compressed sequence will be B=[2,1,2,1,2,1,2,1]B = [2, 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 2, 1]B=[2,1,2,1,2,1,2,1]. The sum of its elements is 121212, which is the minimum.

For a given integer SSS, find the number of values of KKK (0‚â§K‚â§N0 \le K \le N0‚â§K‚â§N) such that the sum of elements of the KKK-compressed sequence does not exceed SSS.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and SSS.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of values of KKK such that the sum of the compressed sequence is ‚â§S\le S‚â§S.
